gettingStarted.title: Getting Started with Tempo.Blazor
gettingStarted.overview: Tempo.Blazor is a comprehensive Blazor WebAssembly component library providing 100+ UI components for building modern web applications. It includes inputs, forms, data tables, pickers, charts, layout, navigation, feedback, workflow designer, scheduler, and more. All components use the Tm prefix (e.g., TmButton, TmDataTable).

packages.name: Tempo.Blazor
packages.description: Main component library with all UI components, CSS, and JS interop.

packages.name: Tempo.Blazor.Abstractions
packages.description: Interfaces and models (zero UI dependencies). Safe to reference from API/service projects for shared contracts like IFileAttachmentProvider, IDashboardProvider, etc.

packages.name: Tempo.Blazor.FluentValidation
packages.description: Optional FluentValidation integration. Provides FluentValidationValidator component and EditContext extensions for async validation.

indexHtml.description: Add CSS and JS references to wwwroot/index.html
indexHtml.code: Loading...

importsRazor.description: Add component namespaces to _Imports.razor for convenient usage without per-file @using directives.

cssFramework.description: Tempo.Blazor uses its own standalone CSS framework based on CSS custom properties (design tokens). No external CSS framework (like Bootstrap or Tailwind) is required. The library ships a single tempo-blazor.bundled.css file that includes all component styles, design tokens, base resets, and animations.
cssFramework.architecture: The CSS is organized as: tokens (design variables) → base resets → component styles. Each component uses BEM-like class naming with the tm- prefix (e.g., tm-btn, tm-data-table, tm-modal__header).

darkMode.description: Dark mode is supported via CSS custom property overrides. Activate by adding data-theme="dark" attribute or .tm-dark class to a parent element (typically <html> or <body>).
darkMode.themeService: Use the ThemeService (registered via DI) to toggle themes programmatically.

designTokens.description: All visual properties are controlled via CSS custom properties (--tm-*). Override these to customize the look and feel without modifying component CSS.

colorPrimary.description: Primary brand color palette (blue by default)

typography.description: Font families, sizes, weights, and line heights

tokens.--tm-font-mono: ui-monospace, 'Cascadia Code', 'Source Code Pro', Menlo, monospace

customization.description: Override design tokens in your app CSS to customize the theme globally.

localization.description: Tempo.Blazor supports localization via ITmLocalizer. Built-in languages: English (en), Czech (cs). Custom localizer can be registered by implementing ITmLocalizer and replacing the default registration.

items.description: Internal helper component that renders the formatting toolbar for TmRichEditorFull and TmRichEditorSimple. Operates in two modes: Simple (basic formatting, lists, links, emojis) and Full (adds headings, strikethrough, subscript/superscript, indent/outdent, code, blockquote, horizontal rule, text/highlight colors, image, table, video, remove format). Not intended for direct use.
Internal modal dialog providing find-and-replace functionality for the rich text editor. Supports single replace and replace-all operations with a result count indicator.
Internal modal dialog for inserting images in the rich text editor. Provides URL and alt text inputs with URL validation and backdrop click to close.
Internal modal dialog for inserting hyperlinks in the rich text editor. Provides URL and display text inputs with URL validation. Supports backdrop click to close and preselected text.

Internal dropdown component for @mention user selection in rich text editors. Displays user avatars, display names, and usernames. Supports keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter), mouse hover highlighting, and query-based filtering.
Internal modal dialog for inserting tables in the rich text editor. Provides numeric row/column inputs (max 20x10) and a visual grid preview for interactive size selection with hover highlighting.
Expandable content panel container that manages child TmAccordionItem components, supporting single or multiple simultaneous expansion.

examples.title: Accordion with multiple items
examples.code:
Content for section 1

Content for section 2

This section cannot be toggled

items.description: Individual expandable panel within a TmAccordion, with a clickable header showing title, optional subtitle, icon, and chevron indicator. Supports keyboard navigation and disabled state.

examples.title: Accordion item with icon and subtitle
examples.code: Account settings content here.

items.description: File attachment manager that lists uploaded files with type-specific icons, file sizes, and uploader names. Supports chunked file upload with progress bar, download via IFileAttachmentProvider, deletion, duplicate detection, and configurable max file size and accepted types.

Comment thread component with inline rich text editing via TmRichEditorSimple. Supports adding, editing, and deleting comments with author avatars, relative timestamps, and edit indicators. Communicates changes via EventCallbacks.

Tabbed activity panel that combines TmActivityTimeline, TmActivityComments, and TmActivityAttachments into a single component with switchable tabs. Each tab can be shown or hidden independently. Tab badges display item counts.

examples.title: Full activity log with all tabs
examples.code:
<TmActivityLog EntityId="@entityId"
               TimelineEntries="@timelineEntries"
               Comments="@comments"
               Attachments="@attachments"
               AttachmentProvider="@attachmentProvider"
               OnCommentAdded="HandleCommentAdded"
               OnCommentDeleted="HandleCommentDeleted" />

items.description: Displays a chronological timeline of activity entries (comments, status changes, assignments, attachments) with author avatars, relative timestamps, and color-coded connector dots per entry type. Entries are sorted newest-first.

examples.title: With ref for programmatic refresh
examples.code:
<TmActivityTimeline @ref="_timelineRef" Entries="@entries" />

@code {
    private TmActivityTimeline? _timelineRef;
    async Task Refresh() => await _timelineRef!.RefreshAsync();
}

items.description: Inline alert banner with severity levels (Info, Success, Warning, Error) and visual variants (Soft, Filled, Outlined). Supports title, description, actions slot, custom icon, and dismissable behavior.

examples.title: Info alert with title
examples.code:
<TmAlert Severity="AlertSeverity.Info" Title="Heads up">
    This is an informational message.
</TmAlert>

examples.title: Dismissable error alert
examples.code:
<TmAlert Severity="AlertSeverity.Error" Variant="AlertVariant.Filled"
    Title="Error" Dismissable="true" OnDismiss="HandleDismiss">
    Something went wrong. Please try again.
</TmAlert>

examples.title: Warning alert with actions
examples.code:
<TmAlert Severity="AlertSeverity.Warning" Variant="AlertVariant.Outlined" Title="Expiring soon">
    Your subscription expires in 3 days.
    <Actions>
        <TmButton Variant="ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="ButtonSize.Sm">Renew now</TmButton>
    </Actions>
</TmAlert>

items.description: Complete attachment management component that combines TmFileDropZone with a list of existing attachments loaded via IFileAttachmentProvider. Supports download, delete, and upload with progress indication.

Displays a user avatar as an image or initials fallback derived from the Name property. Supports six sizes (Xs, Sm, Md, Lg, Xl, Xxl), two shapes (Circle, Square), and multiple background colors for the initials variant.
Renders a horizontal stack of overlapping avatars with an optional overflow indicator showing the count of hidden items (e.g., "+3"). Pass TmAvatar elements as child content.

examples.title: Avatar group with overflow
examples.code:
<TmAvatarGroup Max="3" TotalCount="7" Size="AvatarSize.Sm">
    <TmAvatar Src="/img/user1.jpg" Name="Alice" Size="AvatarSize.Sm" />
    <TmAvatar Src="/img/user2.jpg" Name="Bob" Size="AvatarSize.Sm" />
    <TmAvatar Name="Charlie" Size="AvatarSize.Sm" />
</TmAvatarGroup>

items.description: Inline label element with color variants (Default, Primary, Success, Danger, Warning, Info), style options (Filled, Outline, Subtle), sizes (Sm, Md), optional pill shape, status dot, and icon.

examples.title: Success badge with dot and pill shape

items.description: Breadcrumb navigation trail with customizable separator, icon support, and optional click callback. Items render as links or buttons depending on configuration.
Floating toolbar that appears when items are selected, showing the selection count, custom action buttons via ChildContent, and a clear-selection button.

General-purpose button component with multiple visual variants (Primary, Secondary, Ghost, Danger, Outline, Link, Default), sizes (Xs, Sm, Md, Lg), optional icon, loading state with spinner, and full-width block mode.
Reusable calendar month grid with day selection, range highlighting, today indicator, and month navigation. Used internally by TmDatePicker and TmDateRangePicker.

examples.title: Calendar grid with range selection
examples.code:
<TmCalendarGrid DisplayYear="2026" DisplayMonth="3"
                RangeStart="@rangeStart" RangeEnd="@rangeEnd"
                MinDate="@(new DateOnly(2026, 1, 1))"
                OnDateSelected="HandleDateSelected"
                OnNavigate="HandleNavigate" />

items.description: Full-size calendar view displaying a month grid with event entries, highlighted dates, and click handlers for both dates and events. Suitable for scheduling UIs.

examples.title: Calendar view with highlighted dates and constraints
examples.code:
<TmCalendarView SelectedDate="@selectedDate"
                Events="@events"
                HighlightedDates="@importantDates"
                MinDate="@(new DateOnly(2026, 1, 1))"
                MaxDate="@(new DateOnly(2026, 12, 31))"
                OnDateClick="HandleDateClick" />

items.description: Container card with optional header (text + icon), body content via ChildContent, footer (string or RenderFragment), and three visual variants: Default, Elevated, and Outlined.

examples.title: Card with header and footer
examples.code: $24,500

examples.title: Elevated card with custom footer
examples.code:
Active users: 1,234

 View all users

items.description: Displays a list of property changes in a before/after diff format, showing old values with strikethrough and new values highlighted, connected by an arrow icon.

examples.title: Display property changes
examples.code:
<TmChangeDiff Changes="@(new List<TmChangeInfo>
{
    new() { Property = "Status", OldValue = "Draft", NewValue = "Published" },
    new() { Property = "Title", OldValue = "Old Title", NewValue = "New Title" }
})" />

items.description: Pure SVG chart component supporting Bar, Line, Pie, Donut, and HorizontalBar chart types with optional legend, grid, value labels, animation, and click events on segments.

examples.title: Basic bar chart
examples.code:
<TmChart Type="ChartType.Bar"
         Data="@(new ChartData { Labels = new[] { "Jan", "Feb", "Mar" }, Datasets = new[] { new ChartDataset { Label = "Sales", Values = new[] { 100.0, 200.0, 150.0 }, Color = "#3b82f6" } } })"
         Height="300px"
         ShowLegend="true"
         ShowGrid="true" />

items.description: Checkbox input with label, help text, indeterminate state, and disabled support. Renders a custom styled checkbox with animated check/dash marks.

examples.title: Basic checkbox
examples.code: <TmCheckbox @bind-Value="agreeToTerms" Label="I agree to the terms and conditions" />

examples.title: Checkbox with help text and indeterminate state
examples.code: <TmCheckbox @bind-Value="selectAll" Label="Select all items" HelpText="This will select all visible rows" Indeterminate="@isSomeSelected" />

items.description: Small labelled element with Soft, Filled, and Outlined variants, optional icon, removable close button, clickable/selectable states, custom color via CSS variable, and two sizes.

examples.title: Clickable chip with icon and custom color
examples.code:
<TmChip Label="Priority: High"
        Icon="flag"
        Color="#ef4444"
        Variant="ChipVariant.Filled"
        Clickable="true"
        Selected="@isSelected"
        OnClick="ToggleSelection" />

items.description: Flex-wrap container for grouping multiple TmChip components with consistent spacing and an optional disabled state.

examples.title: Group of chips
examples.code:
<TmChipGroup>
    <TmChip Label="React" Removable="true" OnRemove="() => Remove("React")" />
    <TmChip Label="Blazor" Removable="true" OnRemove="() => Remove("Blazor")" />
    <TmChip Label="Vue" Removable="true" OnRemove="() => Remove("Vue")" />
</TmChipGroup>

items.description: Inline column filter control for TmDataTable that renders a text input, number/date input, boolean select, or custom select dropdown depending on FilterType configuration.
Dropdown panel for toggling column visibility in a TmDataTable, with checkboxes for each hideable column and a reset button to restore defaults.

examples.title: Column picker with toggle and reset

items.description: Searchable command palette overlay (Cmd+K style) with filterable actions, descriptions, and keyboard shortcut hints. Supports two-way binding on open state.
Dropdown context menu triggered by a custom element. Contains TmContextMenuItem children and closes on Escape key press.

examples.title: Context menu with actions
examples.code:
<TmContextMenu>
    <Trigger>
        <TmButton Variant="ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="ButtonSize.Sm">...</TmButton>
    </Trigger>
    <ChildContent>
        <TmContextMenuItem Label="Edit" Icon="edit" OnClick="EditItem" />
        <TmContextMenuItem Label="Duplicate" Icon="copy" OnClick="DuplicateItem" />
        <TmContextMenuItem IsDivider="true" />
        <TmContextMenuItem Label="Delete" Icon="trash-2" IsDangerous="true" OnClick="DeleteItem" />
    </ChildContent>
</TmContextMenu>

items.description: Individual menu item for TmContextMenu. Supports icons, disabled state, danger styling, and divider mode. Automatically closes parent menu on click.
Icon button that copies specified text to the clipboard. Shows a checkmark icon for 2 seconds after successful copy. Uses the browser Clipboard API via JS interop.

Configurable dashboard with a CSS grid layout, drag-and-drop widget positioning, resize handles, edit mode, widget selector modal, multiple saved dashboards via IDashboardProvider, and JS interop for grid interactions.

examples.title: Basic dashboard with provider
examples.code:
<TmDashboard DashboardId="@dashId"
             UserId="@userId"
             ShowTitle="true"
             AllowEdit="true"
             OnDashboardChanged="HandleDashboardChanged">
    <WidgetTemplate Context="widget">
        @switch (widget.WidgetId)
        {
            case "sales-chart":
                <SalesChartWidget />
                break;
            case "recent-orders":
                <RecentOrdersWidget />
                break;
        }
    </WidgetTemplate>
</TmDashboard>

items.description: Full-featured data grid with declarative column definitions, client-side and server-side data modes, sorting, inline column filters, global search, row selection with bulk actions, column visibility picker, drag-to-group with multi-level grouping, pagination, virtualized scrolling, external filter builder, and saved view management.

examples.title: Basic data table with columns
examples.code:
<TmDataTable TItem="Employee" Items="@employees" ViewContext="employees">
    <TmDataTableColumn Title="Name" Field="e => e.Name" Sortable="true" Filterable="true" />
    <TmDataTableColumn Title="Department" Field="e => e.Department" Sortable="true" Groupable="true" />
    <TmDataTableColumn Title="Salary" Field="e => e.Salary" Sortable="true" Align="ColumnAlign.Right" Width="120px" />
    <TmDataTableColumn Title="Actions" Hideable="false">
        <CellTemplate Context="emp">
            <button @onclick="() => Edit(emp)">Edit</button>
        </CellTemplate>
    </TmDataTableColumn>
</TmDataTable>

examples.title: Server-side data table with selection and views
examples.code:
<TmDataTable TItem="OrderDto"
             DataProvider="@orderProvider"
             ViewContext="orders"
             ViewProvider="@viewProvider"
             Selectable="true"
             ShowGrouping="true"
             OnRowClick="HandleRowClick"
             OnSelectionChanged="HandleSelectionChanged">
    <SelectionActions>
        <TmButton OnClick="DeleteSelected">Delete</TmButton>
    </SelectionActions>
    <TmDataTableColumn Title="Order #" Field="o => o.OrderNumber" Sortable="true" />
    <TmDataTableColumn Title="Status" Field="o => o.Status" Filterable="true" FilterType="FilterType.Select" FilterOptions="@statusOptions" />
</TmDataTable>

items.description: Headless column descriptor for TmDataTable that defines header title, field accessor, sorting, filtering, grouping, visibility, alignment, width, custom cell/header templates, and group aggregation.

examples.title: Sortable and filterable column
examples.code:
<TmDataTableColumn Title="Name"
                   PropertyName="FullName"
                   Field="e => e.FullName"
                   Sortable="true"
                   Filterable="true"
                   Groupable="true"
                   Width="200px" />

examples.title: Column with custom cell template
examples.code:
<TmDataTableColumn Title="Status" Field="e => e.Status" Filterable="true" FilterType="FilterType.Select" FilterOptions="@statusOptions">
    <CellTemplate Context="item">
        <TmBadge Variant="GetStatusVariant(item.Status)">@item.Status</TmBadge>
    </CellTemplate>
</TmDataTableColumn>

items.description: Date picker with a trigger button, dropdown calendar popup, today shortcut, clear button, and min/max date constraints. Supports custom date format and label.

examples.title: Date picker with constraints and custom format
examples.code:
<TmDatePicker @bind-Value="selectedDate"
              Label="Start date"
              DateFormat="yyyy-MM-dd"
              MinDate="@(new DateOnly(2026, 1, 1))"
              MaxDate="@(new DateOnly(2026, 12, 31))"
              Required="true" />

items.description: Date range picker with dual side-by-side calendars, range preset buttons (Today, Last Week, Last Month, etc.), hover highlighting, and a two-click start/end selection flow.

examples.title: Date range picker with custom presets
examples.code:
<TmDateRangePicker @bind-Value="dateRange"
                   Label="Filter dates"
                   Presets="@customPresets"
                   MinDate="@(new DateOnly(2025, 1, 1))"
                   DateFormat="yyyy-MM-dd" />

items.description: Combined date and time picker with a calendar popup for the date portion and inline time input. Supports min/max DateTime constraints that cascade between date and time parts.

examples.title: Datetime picker with constraints and seconds

items.description: Full datetime range picker combining two TmDateTimePicker instances for start and end, with cross-constraint validation ensuring end is not before start.

examples.title: Datetime range with constraints and seconds

items.description: Centered alert, confirm, or prompt dialog with severity-based icon and color variants. Prompt mode includes a text input. Supports a custom content mode with FooterContent.

examples.title: Alert dialog
examples.code:
<TmDialog Show="@showAlert" Type="DialogType.Alert" Variant="DialogVariant.Success"
    Title="Success" Message="Record saved successfully."
    OnResult="result => showAlert = false" />

examples.title: Confirm dialog with danger variant
examples.code:
<TmDialog Show="@showConfirm" Type="DialogType.Confirm" IsDangerous="true"
    Title="Delete Item?" Message="This action cannot be undone."
    OkButtonText="Delete" CancelButtonText="Cancel"
    OnResult="HandleConfirmResult" />

examples.title: Prompt dialog
examples.code:
<TmDialog Show="@showPrompt" Type="DialogType.Prompt"
    Title="Rename" Message="Enter a new name:"
    PromptPlaceholder="New name" DefaultValue="@currentName"
    OnPromptResult="HandlePromptResult" />

items.description: Slide-in side panel (left or right) with overlay backdrop, header/body/footer slots, and configurable close behavior via Escape key and overlay click.

examples.title: Basic right-side drawer
examples.code: Drawer body content here.

items.description: Action dropdown menu triggered by a button, supporting icons and keyboard navigation. Items are rendered via TmDropdownItem children.

examples.title: Basic dropdown with items
examples.code:
<TmDropdown Text="Actions" OnSelect="HandleSelect">
    <TmDropdownItem Value="edit" Icon="edit">Edit</TmDropdownItem>
    <TmDropdownItem Value="delete" Icon="trash">Delete</TmDropdownItem>
</TmDropdown>

examples.title: Dropdown with leading icon
examples.code:
<TmDropdown Text="Options" Icon="settings" OnSelect="HandleSelect" Disabled="false">
    <TmDropdownItem Value="export">Export</TmDropdownItem>
    <TmDropdownItem Value="print">Print</TmDropdownItem>
</TmDropdown>

items.description: Individual menu item for TmDropdown. Supports an optional icon, disabled state, and passes its Value to the parent dropdown on click.

Renders a form dynamically from a list of DynamicFieldDefinition objects. Supports text, textarea, number, date, datetime, time, checkbox, and select field types with configurable columns, read-only mode, and two-way value binding.
Centered placeholder shown when a list or section has no content, with optional icon, title, description, and call-to-action button.

examples.title: Empty state with action
examples.code:
<TmEmptyState Title="No results found"
              Description="Try adjusting your filters or search terms."
              Icon="search"
              ActionText="Clear Filters"
              OnAction="ClearFilters" />

items.description: Searchable entity lookup input that calls an async search provider after a configurable debounce, displays results in a dropdown, and binds to a typed value upon selection.

examples.title: Entity picker for users
examples.code: <TmEntityPicker TItem="UserDto" TValue="Guid" @bind-Value="selectedUserId" SearchProvider="@SearchUsersAsync" ValueSelector="@(u => u.Id)" DisplaySelector="@(u => u.FullName)" Label="Assigned To" Placeholder="Search users..." />

examples.title: Entity picker with custom debounce and min search length
examples.code: <TmEntityPicker TItem="CompanyDto" TValue="int" @bind-Value="companyId" SearchProvider="@SearchCompaniesAsync" ValueSelector="@(c => c.Id)" DisplaySelector="@(c => c.Name)" Label="Company" MinSearchLength="3" Debounce="500" />

items.description: Export configuration panel with entity type checkboxes, format selector dropdown, and an export action button that emits a TmExportRequest with the selected format and entities.
Template expression editor with a textarea and a side panel of clickable variables that insert {{variableName}} tokens into the expression text.

examples.title: Expression editor with variables
examples.code: <TmExpressionEditor @bind-Value="template" Label="Email Template" Placeholder="Type your expression..." Variables="@variables" />

items.description: Drag-and-drop file upload zone with built-in file browser, file list display with icons and sizes, removable items, and support for multiple files and accept filters.

examples.title: Custom drop zone content
examples.code: Drop your CSV files here

items.description: Generic searchable dropdown with client-side or server-side data loading, keyboard navigation, custom item templates, and a clear button. Supports two-way binding via Value/ValueChanged.

examples.title: Client-side filterable dropdown
examples.code:
<TmFilterableDropdown TItem="string" TValue="string"
    Items="@countries"
    @bind-Value="selectedCountry"
    Placeholder="Select country"
    ShowClearButton="true" />

examples.title: Server-side data provider with custom template
examples.code: @context.FullName (@context.Email)

items.description: Interactive filter builder UI that lets users add, edit, and remove filters using field definitions. Renders active filters as TmFilterChip components with a field picker, operator selector, and value input. Supports text, number, date, boolean, select, and multi-select field types.

examples.title: Filter definitions setup

items.description: Compact pill displaying an active filter with field label and value. Supports remove and edit click callbacks.
Generic form field wrapper providing a label (with optional required marker), help text, error message display, and a slot for any form control. Applies error styling when an error message is set.

examples.title: Form field with label and help text
examples.code:
<TmFormField Label="Username" Required="true" HelpText="Must be at least 3 characters." For="username">
    <input id="username" type="text" @bind="model.Username" />
</TmFormField>

items.description: Grid-based form row that arranges child content into 1 to 4 equal-width columns.

examples.title: Two-column form row
examples.code:
<TmFormRow Columns="2">
    <TmFormField Label="First Name"><input type="text" /></TmFormField>
    <TmFormField Label="Last Name"><input type="text" /></TmFormField>
</TmFormRow>

examples.title: Three-column layout
examples.code:
<TmFormRow Columns="3">
    <TmFormField Label="City"><input type="text" /></TmFormField>
    <TmFormField Label="State"><input type="text" /></TmFormField>
    <TmFormField Label="ZIP"><input type="text" /></TmFormField>
</TmFormRow>

items.description: Collapsible form section with a title header, optional description, and a body area for grouping related form fields.

examples.title: Basic form section
examples.code:
<TmFormSection Title="Personal Information" Description="Enter your basic details.">
    <TmFormField Label="Name"><input type="text" /></TmFormField>
    <TmFormField Label="Email"><input type="email" /></TmFormField>
</TmFormSection>

examples.title: Collapsible section, collapsed by default
examples.code:
<TmFormSection Title="Advanced Settings" Collapsible="true" CollapsedByDefault="true">
    <TmFormField Label="API Key"><input type="text" /></TmFormField>
</TmFormSection>

items.description: Displays field-level validation error messages from a cascading EditContext for a specific field expression, with an error icon for each message.
SVG icon component with 150+ built-in icons (Feather-style), five size variants (Xs–Xl), six color variants, and support for custom icons via IconRegistry.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Icon name. Use IconNames constants (e.g. IconNames.Check) or a custom registered name.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Icon size: Xs (0.75rem), Sm (1rem), Md (1.25rem), Lg (1.5rem), Xl (2rem).

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: Icon color: Current (inherits), Primary, Danger, Success, Warning, Muted.

examples.title: Register a custom icon (in Program.cs)
examples.description: Register inline SVG content (inner elements only, NOT the <svg> wrapper). Call before app.Run().

examples.title: Register multiple custom icons
examples.description: You can register as many custom icons as needed. Names are case-insensitive.
examples.code:
IconRegistry.Register("company-logo", "<circle cx='12' cy='12' r='10'/><path d='M8 12h8'/>");
IconRegistry.Register("custom-arrow", "<line x1='12' y1='5' x2='12' y2='19'/><polyline points='19 12 12 19 5 12'/>");

examples.title: Use a registered custom icon in Razor
Register a custom IIconProvider (in Program.cs)
examples.description: Implement IIconProvider to provide a whole set of icons (e.g. from a font or sprite sheet). Providers are consulted after the direct registry, in registration order.

notes: Icon names use IconNames constants (e.g. IconNames.Check) to avoid typos.
Edit and Pencil are aliases pointing to the same 'edit' icon.
Custom icons registered via IconRegistry.Register() must contain only inner SVG elements (path, circle, line, etc.) — NOT the <svg> wrapper.
Resolution order: built-in icons → direct registry → registered IIconProvider providers.
If an icon name is not found anywhere, the component renders a <span class='tm-icon-unknown'> fallback.

items.description: Responsive image gallery grid with integrated lightbox viewer, loading state, empty state, and optional image deletion via a data provider.

examples.title: Gallery with loading state and delete support
examples.code: <TmImageGallery Images="@images" IsLoading="@isLoading" CanDelete="true" DataProvider="@galleryProvider" OnImageClick="@HandleImageClick" EmptyTitle="No photos yet" />

items.description: Data import preview panel with title, description, content area for displaying imported data, and confirm/cancel action buttons with loading state.

examples.title: Import preview with data table
examples.code:
<TmImportPreview Title="Preview Import" Description="Review the data below before confirming." OnConfirm="@HandleConfirm" OnCancel="@HandleCancel" IsLoading="@isImporting">
    <TmDataTable Items="@previewRows" />
</TmImportPreview>

examples.title: Import preview with custom button text
examples.code: @previewRows.Count rows will be imported.

items.description: Multi-step import wizard with a step indicator (via TmStepper), back/next/cancel/complete navigation buttons, and customizable button text. Steps are defined as child TmImportWizardStep components.

examples.title: Three-step import wizard
examples.code:
Upload your CSV file.

Map file columns to fields.

Review and confirm import.

items.description: Individual step definition for TmImportWizard. Only renders its content when the step is active. Provides title, description, and icon metadata for the step indicator.

examples.title: Wizard step with description
examples.code:
<TmImportWizardStep Title="Configure" Description="Set import options" Icon="settings">
    <TmSelect TValue="string" @bind-Value="delimiter" Label="Delimiter" Options="@delimiterOptions" />
</TmImportWizardStep>

items.description: Click-to-edit inline text component. Displays a read-only value that switches to an input on click. Supports Enter to save, Escape to cancel, optional validation, and placeholder text.

examples.title: Basic inline edit
examples.code:
<TmInlineEdit Value="@projectName" OnSave="name => projectName = name"
    Placeholder="Click to enter name" />

examples.title: Inline edit with validation
examples.code:
<TmInlineEdit Value="@title" OnSave="HandleSave"
    Validate="@(val => val.Length < 3 ? "Must be at least 3 characters" : null)" />

items.description: Notification bell icon button with unread badge count and a dropdown panel showing notification items with severity icons, relative timestamps, and mark-as-read actions. Supports limiting visible items via MaxVisible parameter.

parameters.name: OnNotificationClick
parameters.description: Fires when a notification item is clicked.

parameters.name: OnMarkAsRead
parameters.description: Fires when mark-as-read is clicked for a single notification. Argument is the item Id.

parameters.name: OnMarkAllRead
parameters.description: Fires when the Mark all as read button is clicked.

parameters.name: MaxVisible
parameters.description: Maximum number of notifications shown in the dropdown.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: When true the bell button is disabled.

examples.title: Basic notification bell with all callbacks
Full usage with @code block
examples.code:
<TmNotificationBell Notifications="notifications"
                    OnNotificationClick="HandleClick"
                    OnMarkAsRead="HandleMarkAsRead"
                    OnMarkAllRead="HandleMarkAllRead"
                    MaxVisible="8" />

@code {
    private List<INotificationItem> notifications = new();

    private void HandleClick(INotificationItem item)
    {
        // Navigate or show detail
    }

    private void HandleMarkAsRead(string id)
    {
        var n = notifications.FirstOrDefault(x => x.Id == id);
        if (n is not null) n.IsRead = true;
    }

    private void HandleMarkAllRead()
    {
        foreach (var n in notifications) n.IsRead = true;
    }
}

items.description: Interactive SVG canvas for designing state-machine workflows. Renders states as shaped nodes (pill for initial, double-border for final, rounded rect for intermediate) connected by curved transition arrows. Supports drag-to-move nodes, drag-from-port to create transitions, zoom/pan via JS interop, context menus, grid snapping, and read-only mode.

examples.title: Editable workflow designer
examples.code: <TmWorkflowDesignerCanvas Definition="@_workflow" DefinitionChanged="@(def => _workflow = def)" OnStateSelected="@(s => _selectedState = s)" OnTransitionSelected="@(t => _selectedTransition = t)" SelectedStateId="@_selectedState?.Id" SelectedTransitionId="@_selectedTransition?.Id" />

items.description: Side panel for editing properties of a selected workflow state or transition. Shows name/label input, state type radio buttons, color swatch picker, and from/to display for transitions. Supports read-only mode.
Side panel toolbox for the workflow designer providing buttons to add initial, intermediate, and final states, delete selected elements, and zoom in/out/fit-to-view controls.

items.description: Internal dropdown component for {{token}} variable selection in rich text editors. Displays token keys, descriptions, and optional category grouping with sticky section headers. Supports keyboard navigation, query-based filtering, and an optional "Create new token" action at the bottom.

examples.title: Used internally by rich editors
examples.code: <TokenAutocomplete IsVisible="true" Tokens="@tokens" Query="@query" HighlightedIndex="0" SupportsCreation="true" OnTokenSelected="HandleSelected" OnCreateRequested="HandleCreate" OnHighlightedIndexChanged="HandleIndexChanged" />

items.description: Internal modal dialog for embedding videos in the rich text editor. Accepts YouTube, Vimeo, or direct video URLs with an optional title field. URLs are validated before insertion.

libraryExamples.title: FluentValidation - Complete Form
libraryExamples.description: Full form with FluentValidation, TmValidatedField for automatic error display, TmValidationSummary, and form sections with two-column layout.

libraryExamples.title: DataAnnotations Validation
libraryExamples.description: Form using standard DataAnnotations with TmFormValidationMessage for field-level errors and TmValidationSummary for a top-level error summary.
libraryExamples.code:
<EditForm Model="@_model" OnValidSubmit="HandleSubmit">
    <DataAnnotationsValidator />

    <TmValidationSummary />

    <TmFormRow Columns="2">
        <TmFormField Label="Username" Required="true">
            <TmTextInput @bind-Value="_model.Username" Placeholder="Choose a username" />
            <TmFormValidationMessage For="@(() => _model.Username)" />
        </TmFormField>
        <TmFormField Label="Email" Required="true">
            <TmTextInput @bind-Value="_model.Email" Type="email" LeftIcon="@IconNames.Mail" />
            <TmFormValidationMessage For="@(() => _model.Email)" />
        </TmFormField>
    </TmFormRow>

    <TmFormField Label="Age" Required="true">
        <TmNumberInput @bind-Value="_model.Age" Min="0" Max="150" />
        <TmFormValidationMessage For="@(() => _model.Age)" />
    </TmFormField>

    <TmButton Type="ButtonType.Submit" Variant="ButtonVariant.Primary">Register</TmButton>
</EditForm>

@code {
    private RegistrationModel _model = new();
    private async Task HandleSubmit() { /* save */ }
}

libraryExamples.title: Programmatic FluentValidation (without component)
libraryExamples.description: Using EditContext.AddFluentValidation() extension method for programmatic validation without the FluentValidationValidator component. Useful when you need to create EditContext manually.
libraryExamples.setupNote: Requires: @using Tempo.Blazor.FluentValidation and the AddFluentValidation extension method from EditContextFluentValidationExtensions.

libraryExamples.title: CSS Design Tokens - Custom Theme
libraryExamples.description: Override Tempo.Blazor design tokens to create a custom brand theme. Place these overrides in your app's CSS file, loaded after tempo-blazor.css.

libraryExamples.title: CSS Design Tokens - Dark Mode Toggle
libraryExamples.description: Implement dark mode toggle using ThemeService and the built-in dark theme tokens. ThemeService is a simple in-memory service with IsDark, ThemeName, Toggle(), and an OnChanged event.
libraryExamples.setupNote: The dark theme is defined in tokens-dark.css and activates when [data-theme="dark"] or .tm-dark class is set on a parent element. ThemeService has: IsDark (bool), ThemeName ("dark"/"light"), Toggle(), OnChanged (event). Register in Program.cs: builder.Services.AddScoped<ThemeService>(); For app-wide dark mode, set data-theme on <html> or your root layout element.

libraryExamples.title: CSS Design Tokens - Component-Scoped Overrides
libraryExamples.description: Override design tokens for specific sections of your page using CSS custom property scoping.
libraryExamples.code: Upgrade Now PRO

libraryExamples.title: Data Table with CRUD Operations
libraryExamples.description: Full data management page with TmDataTable, toolbar actions, row selection, bulk actions, filter builder, and a drawer form for create/edit.
libraryExamples.code:
@page "/employees"

<TmToolbar Title="Employees" Sticky="true">
    <Actions>
        <TmToolbarButton Icon="@IconNames.Filter" Text="Filters"
            OnClick="() => _showFilters = !_showFilters" />
        <TmToolbarButton Icon="@IconNames.Download" Text="Export"
            OnClick="HandleExport" />
        <TmToolbarDivider />
        <TmToolbarButton Icon="@IconNames.Plus" Text="New Employee"
            Variant="ButtonVariant.Primary" OnClick="OpenCreateForm" />
    </Actions>
</TmToolbar>

@if (_showFilters)
{
    <TmFilterBuilder FilterDefinitions="@_filterDefs" ActiveFilters="@_activeFilters"
        OnFiltersChanged="HandleFiltersChanged" ShowClearAll="true" />
}

<TmDataTable TItem="EmployeeDto" DataProvider="@_dataProvider" ViewContext="employees"
    Selectable="true" ShowGrouping="true"
    OnRowClick="OpenEditForm" OnSelectionChanged="sel => _selected = sel.ToList()">
    <SelectionActions>
        <TmButton Variant="ButtonVariant.Ghost" OnClick="ExportSelected">
            <TmIcon Name="@IconNames.Download" Size="IconSize.Sm" /> Export
        </TmButton>
        <TmButton Variant="ButtonVariant.Danger" OnClick="ConfirmDeleteSelected">
            <TmIcon Name="@IconNames.Trash2" Size="IconSize.Sm" /> Delete
        </TmButton>
    </SelectionActions>

    <TmDataTableColumn Title="Name" Field="e => e.FullName" Sortable="true"
        Filterable="true" Groupable="true" />
    <TmDataTableColumn Title="Department" Field="e => e.Department" Sortable="true"
        Filterable="true" FilterType="FilterType.Select"
        FilterOptions="@_deptOptions" Groupable="true" />
    <TmDataTableColumn Title="Status" Field="e => e.Status">
        <CellTemplate Context="emp">
            <TmBadge Variant="@GetStatusVariant(emp.Status)"
                BadgeStyle="BadgeStyle.Subtle" Dot="true">@emp.Status</TmBadge>
        </CellTemplate>
    </TmDataTableColumn>
    <TmDataTableColumn Title="Hired" Field="e => e.HireDate" Sortable="true"
        Align="ColumnAlign.Right" Width="120px" />
    <TmDataTableColumn Title="" Width="50px" Hideable="false">
        <CellTemplate Context="emp">
            <TmContextMenu>
                <Trigger>
                    <TmButton Variant="ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="ButtonSize.Sm">
                        <TmIcon Name="@IconNames.MoreVertical" Size="IconSize.Sm" />
                    </TmButton>
                </Trigger>
                <ChildContent>
                    <TmContextMenuItem Label="Edit" Icon="@IconNames.Edit"
                        OnClick="() => OpenEditForm(emp)" />
                    <TmContextMenuItem Label="Duplicate" Icon="@IconNames.Copy"
                        OnClick="() => Duplicate(emp)" />
                    <TmContextMenuItem IsDivider="true" />
                    <TmContextMenuItem Label="Delete" Icon="@IconNames.Trash2"
                        IsDangerous="true" OnClick="() => ConfirmDelete(emp)" />
                </ChildContent>
            </TmContextMenu>
        </CellTemplate>
    </TmDataTableColumn>
</TmDataTable>

<TmDrawer @bind-IsOpen="_drawerOpen" Title="@(_isNew ? "New Employee" : "Edit Employee")" Width="600px">
    <EditForm Model="@_formModel" OnValidSubmit="SaveEmployee">
        <FluentValidationValidator />
        <TmValidationSummary />

        <TmFormRow Columns="2">
            <TmValidatedField Label="First Name" @bind-Value="_formModel.FirstName" Required="true" />
            <TmValidatedField Label="Last Name" @bind-Value="_formModel.LastName" Required="true" />
        </TmFormRow>
        <TmValidatedField Label="Email" @bind-Value="_formModel.Email" Required="true" LeftIcon="@IconNames.Mail" />
        <TmFormField Label="Department">
            <TmSelect TValue="string" @bind-Value="_formModel.Department" Options="@_deptSelectOptions" />
        </TmFormField>
        <TmFormField Label="Hire Date">
            <TmDatePicker @bind-Value="_formModel.HireDate" />
        </TmFormField>
    </EditForm>
    <FooterContent>
        <TmButton Variant="ButtonVariant.Primary" OnClick="SaveEmployee" IsLoading="@_isSaving">Save</TmButton>
        <TmButton Variant="ButtonVariant.Ghost" OnClick="() => _drawerOpen = false">Cancel</TmButton>
    </FooterContent>
</TmDrawer>

<TmDialog @bind-Show="_showDeleteConfirm" Type="DialogType.Confirm" IsDangerous="true"
    Title="Delete Employee?" Message="This action cannot be undone."
    OkButtonText="Delete" OnResult="HandleDeleteResult" />

libraryExamples.title: Application Shell with Navigation
libraryExamples.description: Full application layout combining TmTopBar, TmSidebar, TmBreadcrumbs, TmDrawer, TmToastContainer, TmCommandPalette, and TmNotificationBell.

libraryExamples.title: Activity Panel with Rich Text Comments
libraryExamples.description: Tabbed activity panel combining timeline, rich text comments with @mentions and {{tokens}}, and file attachments.
libraryExamples.code:
<TmTabs @bind-ActiveTabId="_activeTab" Variant="TabVariant.Line">
    <TmTabPanel Id="timeline" Title="Activity" Icon="@IconNames.Activity">
        <TmActivityTimeline Entries="@_timelineEntries" />
    </TmTabPanel>

    <TmTabPanel Id="comments" Title="Comments" Icon="@IconNames.MessageCircle"
        Badge="@_comments.Count.ToString()">
        <TmActivityComments Comments="@_comments"
            OnCommentAdded="HandleCommentAdd"
            OnCommentEdited="HandleCommentEdit"
            OnCommentDeleted="HandleCommentDelete"
            ShowAddButton="true" />
    </TmTabPanel>

    <TmTabPanel Id="files" Title="Files" Icon="@IconNames.Paperclip"
        Badge="@_attachments.Count.ToString()">
        <TmActivityAttachments EntityId="@_entityId"
            Provider="@_attachmentProvider"
            Attachments="@_attachments"
            AllowUpload="true"
            MaxFileSizeBytes="52428800"
            AcceptedFileTypes=".pdf,.docx,.xlsx,.jpg,.png" />
    </TmTabPanel>
</TmTabs>

@* Or use the all-in-one TmActivityLog component: *@
<TmActivityLog EntityId="@_entityId"
    TimelineEntries="@_timelineEntries"
    Comments="@_comments"
    Attachments="@_attachments"
    AttachmentProvider="@_attachmentProvider"
    OnCommentAdded="HandleCommentAdd"
    OnCommentDeleted="HandleCommentDelete" />

libraryExamples.title: Custom Icon Registration
libraryExamples.description: Register custom application-specific icons via IconRegistry in Program.cs. Icons can be registered individually with inline SVG content, or in bulk via a custom IIconProvider implementation.
libraryExamples.usageCode:
@* Use registered custom icons in Razor — reference by the registered name *@
<TmIcon Name="company-logo" Size="IconSize.Lg" />
<TmIcon Name="app-dashboard" Color="IconColor.Primary" />
<TmIcon Name="app-reports" Size="IconSize.Xl" StrokeWidth="1.5" />
libraryExamples.setupNote: Icon resolution order: built-in icons → direct registry (IconRegistry.Register) → IIconProvider providers (in registration order). SVG content must be inner elements only (path, circle, line, rect, polyline, etc.) — NOT the <svg> wrapper. Names are case-insensitive.
